MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that Ms. Caldwell has not addressed the examiner's notes, that she called in this morning to say that she cannot appear and that she has done this on prior occasions. An Order to Appear will be issued to the administrator Karen Caldwell to appear on January 9, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the has not responded to the examiner's notes. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Continued To Sep-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell in Propria Persona.

The Court asked counsel to address the items in the examiner's notes and that status on the outstanding items. Ms. Caldwell responded that she has statements with her, that the beneficiaries really wanted their checks in hand but were advised that they could not cash them until the order was signed, they cashed the checks anyway, one of the beneficiaries told counsel that probate was not necessary as the estate was under $150,000.00, that she will file receipts as well as all statements, that there will still be the issue with Bank of America, that the beneficiaries are older folks who do not have email and that she has struggled to get them to cooperate. The Court stated that it wants to make sure that it has everything and asked about the notice of hearing and proof of service, the supplement to the petition, the original bank statements and the receipt from the Carmelite Monastery. Ms. Caldwell responded in that she will have the beneficiaries sign waivers of notice, that she did file a supplement and will file an amended supplement, that she has bank statements as of 4/30/16 and will bring in statements through July and that she will go over to the Carmelite Monastery and pick up the receipt. Ms. Caldwell further reported that Bank of America has not changed its position, that it is believed the documents were lost, that they changed the identification, that one clerk said to her "I don't know what it is but will treat it as a pay on death", that Bank of America says they have the ability to determine what the account is but will not provide proof, that Chris Patsilelis would like to pursue it since he did not get his 1/2, that someone will have to file some kind of action against Bank of America and that the beneficiary who received the money does not want to return it. The Court asked Ms. Caldwell for a time estimate on submitting the outstanding documents. Ms. Caldwell requested 3 weeks, stated that she would like instruction regarding Bank of America, that she wants the beneficiaries to be responsible for pursuing any action and that she will include this in a proposed order. The accounting petition was continued to September 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the outstanding items and the petition for final distribution.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Continued To Jun-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for petitionerAlice Traeg

Ms. Caldwell reported that there were two matters which held up the accounting. The first was the failure of a beneficiary to return a signed receipt for a distribution payment of $100. The other was a problem she had experienced regarding the Bank of America account. BofA requested that she consent to the account being treated as a payable upon death account, but the bank failed to provide her with any documentation showing the account to be payable on death so she refused to give the requested consent believing the account to be an asset of the estate. Despite her refusal, the Bank took the position that there was an ambiguity regarding the account which the bank had the power to determine, and that the bank had made the payment of the account over her objection. She contended that this was an error by the bank, perhaps due to the accout at one time being a joint account. She is still disputing this payment by the bank, but would like to put the matter to rest. She has agreed to waive her fee in the matter. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported)

Off Calendar May-09-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Continued To Sep-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for and with Alice Brown Traeg.

Ms. Caldwell reported that she filed a declaration addressing the court's concerns, that they can set status hearings for benchmark due dates, that this is a small estate and is all cash and that the funds can be deposited into an estate account requiring two signatures. The Court stated that having reviewed the petition it is not inclined to appoint Ms. Traeg. Ms. Caldwell stated that the safeguards will provide adequate protection, that she has spoken with both beneficiaries, that they are aware of the history and agree to move forward, that Ms. Traeg has been monitored and is in a program with the bar and that there have been no issues. The Court stated that it is not convinced and that the matter will be continued to provide time to amend the petition nominating a new administrator. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to September 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
